pattern: A single global digit-for-digit substitution is decided by which digit position carries the most positional weight: the leading digit for shrinking the number, the first sub-maximal digit for growing it.
hints: You get to pick one digit value and replace every one of its occurrences in the number with another digit value, a single global swap.

To grow the number as much as possible, look at the digits from the left and find the first one that is not already the largest possible digit. To shrink it, focus on the digit that carries the most weight in the number: the leading one.

Turn every copy of the first non-nine digit (scanning left to right) into nine for the maximum, and turn every copy of the leading digit into zero for the minimum; the answer is the difference of the two resulting numbers.

edge_inputs.description: single digit at the low end of the swap range
repeated leading digit across most of the number
all nines just under a power of ten
leading digit reappears later, remapping it zeroes out more than one place
the digit that must grow is not the leading one
growing digit and shrinking digit are different single digits

stress.description: uniformly random values across the full allowed range
stress.note: size is ignored; a single random integer exercises the full domain

stress.description: values built from few distinct repeated digits, stressing the global-remap logic
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    length = rng.randint(1, 9)
    digits = [rng.choice('0123456789') for _ in range(2)]
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(digits) for _ in range(length))
    s = s.lstrip('0') or '0'
    if s == '0':
        s = '1'
    return [int(s) if int(s) >= 1 else 1]
